signals.title: GBP/AUD — G8 Bilateral Theme + BoE Growth Warning
signals.text: GBP/AUD spread at 1.200% marks a critical G8 divergence as GBP outperforms (+0.637%) while AUD lags (-0.563%). Despite BoE Governor Bailey noting a 'softening picture for growth,' GBP remains the weekly leader. AUD is pressured by a -60bp carry deficit vs USD and a sharp -6.25% drop in WTI Oil, weighing on the commodity-linked currency. The 1.200% spread confirms a strong bilateral theme favoring GBP over AUD despite dovish BoE rhetoric.

signals.title: EUR/USD — Headline + Volatility + Positioning
signals.text: EUR/USD at 1.1625 as ECB sources signal a June rate hike is 'nearly sealed,' yet the pair remains down -0.99% on the month. Options markets show an elevated +2.2% Vol Risk Premium (IV 7.1% vs HV30 4.9%), indicating heavy hedging demand against the 175bp USD carry advantage. COT data shows specs are net short (lev_net -8,151), suggesting the market is fading the hawkish ECB shift. Watch for a break of the 1.1600 handle to confirm if carry dominance overrides the ECB's hiking bias.

signals.title: AUD/NZD — Retail vs COT Divergence
signals.text: AUD/NZD retail sentiment has reached an extreme 87% short, directly diverging from the heavy institutional long bias in AUD (COT long% 74.8%). This 13/87 retail split at 1.2183 (implied) suggests extreme retail fading of the AUD strength. Given the RBA's 4.35% hiking stance vs RBNZ's 2.25% hold, the +210bp carry advantage supports the institutional long. Retail capitulation risk is high if AUD/NZD extends gains toward the next RBNZ meeting (mkt: 20% hike priced) on 27 May.

signals.title: USD/JPY — Carry + Options Skew
signals.text: USD/JPY at 158.89 remains supported by a +280bp OIS carry advantage (SOFR 3.53% vs TONA 0.73%). While the pair is testing the 159.00 level, the 25d Risk Reversal shows a moderate -1.44 put skew, signaling that the options market is paying a premium for JPY protection (downside USD/JPY) despite the carry bid. This reflects persistent intervention fears as price holds near the 160.00 psychological barrier. Carry dominates the spot move while VIX remains at 17.6 (normal volatility).
